issues.title: Users: add user-note helpers (add_user_note / list_user_notes / remove_user_note)
issues.body_raw:
**Domain:** Users
**Priority:** Medium
**Effort:** S (≤½ day)

## Background

Alma stores user notes inside the user object itself (\`user_note\` is a sub-field of the user dict returned by \`GET /almaws/v1/users/{user_id}\`), not on a dedicated endpoint. Today the toolkit has no convenience methods for managing notes; callers must do the read-mutate-write dance manually:

\`\`\`python
user = users.get_user(user_id)
user.setdefault('user_note', {}).setdefault('user_note', []).append({
    'note_type': {'value': 'CIRCULATION'},
    'note_text': 'Returned damaged book on 2026-05-08',
    'user_viewable': False,
    'popup_note': False,
})
users.update_user(user_id, user)
\`\`\`

That's verbose and error-prone (the wrapping shape \`user_note.user_note: list\` is non-obvious; the field-level dicts use Alma's \`{value: ..., desc: ...}\` idiom for \`note_type\`).

## Methods to add

\`\`\`python
def list_user_notes(self, user_id: str) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
    \"\"\"Return the existing notes on a user. Empty list if none.\"\"\"

def add_user_note(
    self,
    user_id: str,
    note_text: str,
    note_type: str = 'CIRCULATION',
    user_viewable: bool = False,
    popup_note: bool = False,
) -> AlmaResponse:
    \"\"\"Append a note to the user's note list. Reads the user, appends, PUTs.\"\"\"

def remove_user_notes(
    self,
    user_id: str,
    predicate: Callable[[Dict[str, Any]], bool],
) -> AlmaResponse:
    \"\"\"Remove every note matching the predicate. Returns the PUT response.\"\"\"
\`\`\`

Open question: whether to support \`update_user_note(user_id, index, ...)\`. Alma doesn't have a stable note id, so update-by-index is fragile in the face of concurrent edits. A predicate-based remove + add-new is safer than in-place update.

## Files to touch

- \`src/almaapitk/domains/users.py\` — add the three methods.
- \`tests/unit/domains/test_users.py\` — unit tests with mocked HTTP.

## Acceptance criteria

- All three methods on the existing \`Users\` class with type hints + Google-style docstrings.
- \`add_user_note\` validates \`user_id\` (non-empty string) and \`note_text\` (non-empty string). Allowed \`note_type\` values per Alma docs (\`CIRCULATION\`, \`OTHER\`, etc.) — accept any non-empty string and let Alma reject invalid values.
- \`list_user_notes\` validates \`user_id\` and returns \`[]\` if the user has no notes.
- \`remove_user_notes\` validates \`user_id\` and \`predicate\`. Documents the read-mutate-write semantics so callers know it's a full PUT, not a partial update.
- All ops log through \`self.logger\`.
- Unit-test coverage (mocked HTTP) for each method, including the wrapping-shape edge cases (\`user_note.user_note\` may be missing, may be a single dict instead of a list, etc.).
- Live SANDBOX integration test (separate chunk via the chunks pipeline) that round-trips: read, add, list (assert added), remove-by-predicate, list (assert empty).

## Notes for the implementing agent

- The Alma user response shape for notes is wrapped: \`user.user_note.user_note\` is the array. When the array has a single element, Alma sometimes serializes it as a single dict instead of a list — the read/list/remove paths should normalise.
- The PUT endpoint is the same \`PUT /almaws/v1/users/{user_id}\` used by \`update_user\`. Reuse the existing \`update_user\` rather than calling \`client.put\` directly.
acceptance_criteria: All three methods on the existing \`Users\` class with type hints + Google-style docstrings.

\`add_user_note\` validates \`user_id\` (non-empty string) and \`note_text\` (non-empty string). Allowed \`note_type\` values per Alma docs (\`CIRCULATION\`, \`OTHER\`, etc.) — accept any non-empty string and let Alma reject invalid values.
\`list_user_notes\` validates \`user_id\` and returns \`[]\` if the user has no notes.
\`remove_user_notes\` validates \`user_id\` and \`predicate\`. Documents the read-mutate-write semantics so callers know it's a full PUT, not a partial update.
Unit-test coverage (mocked HTTP) for each method, including the wrapping-shape edge cases (\`user_note.user_note\` may be missing, may be a single dict instead of a list, etc.).
Live SANDBOX integration test (separate chunk via the chunks pipeline) that round-trips: read, add, list (assert added), remove-by-predicate, list (assert empty).
